item.title: Father Gets Revenge On Live TV! Jeff Doucet Case
item.description: In 1984, Gary Plauche shot and killed Jeff Doucet at a Baton Rouge airport as Doucet was being transported by police. Doucet had been accused of kidnapping and assaulting Plauche’s son. The incident was captured by a news crew on live TV. Plauche received a suspended sentence and probation for the shooting.

media:content.media:title: Father Gets Revenge On Live TV! Jeff Doucet Case
media:content.media:description: In 1984, Gary Plauche shot and killed Jeff Doucet at a Baton Rouge airport as Doucet was being transported by police. Doucet had been accused of kidnapping and assaulting Plauche’s son. The incident was captured by a news crew on live TV. Plauche received a suspended sentence and probation for the shooting.

item.title: The Mysterious Vanishing of Ali Lowitzer
item.description: In April 2010, 16-year-old Ali Lowitzer vanished after getting off her school bus in Spring, Texas. She was last seen walking toward her place of work to collect a paycheck. Despite extensive searches by the FBI and local police, no forensic evidence or suspects have been identified. The case remains an active investigation.

media:content.media:title: The Mysterious Vanishing of Ali Lowitzer
media:content.media:description: In April 2010, 16-year-old Ali Lowitzer vanished after getting off her school bus in Spring, Texas. She was last seen walking toward her place of work to collect a paycheck. Despite extensive searches by the FBI and local police, no forensic evidence or suspects have been identified. The case remains an active investigation.

item.title: The Horrifying Secrets of Robert Pickton's Pig Farm
item.description:
In 2007, a jury in British Columbia convicted pig farmer Robert Pickton of six counts of second-degree murder. The investigation began in 2002 when police executed a search warrant related to firearms offenses at his Port Coquitlam property. Forensic teams subsequently identified the remains or DNA of several missing women on the site.
Pickton was originally charged with the deaths of 26 women, though many charges were stayed after his initial conviction resulted in a life sentence. The case drew significant attention to the disappearances of women from Vancouver’s Downtown Eastside over several decades. It led to a provincial inquiry regarding police conduct and the handling of missing person reports.
The legal proceedings concluded with Pickton receiving the maximum sentence of life imprisonment with no possibility of parole for 25 years. Authorities eventually demolished the farm buildings and cleared the site following the conclusion of the forensic investigation. Pickton remained in federal custody until his death following an inmate assault in May 2024.

media:content.media:title: The Horrifying Secrets of Robert Pickton's Pig Farm
media:content.media:description:
In 2007, a jury in British Columbia convicted pig farmer Robert Pickton of six counts of second-degree murder. The investigation began in 2002 when police executed a search warrant related to firearms offenses at his Port Coquitlam property. Forensic teams subsequently identified the remains or DNA of several missing women on the site.
Pickton was originally charged with the deaths of 26 women, though many charges were stayed after his initial conviction resulted in a life sentence. The case drew significant attention to the disappearances of women from Vancouver’s Downtown Eastside over several decades. It led to a provincial inquiry regarding police conduct and the handling of missing person reports.
The legal proceedings concluded with Pickton receiving the maximum sentence of life imprisonment with no possibility of parole for 25 years. Authorities eventually demolished the farm buildings and cleared the site following the conclusion of the forensic investigation. Pickton remained in federal custody until his death following an inmate assault in May 2024.

item.title: Husband convicted in New York murder for hire plot
item.description: In 2015, authorities found Kelley Clayton deceased in her Caton home. An investigation revealed her husband, Thomas Clayton, recruited Michael Beard to carry out the crime. Both men were convicted of first degree murder and sentenced to life in prison without parole.

media:content.media:title: Husband convicted in New York murder for hire plot
media:content.media:description: In 2015, authorities found Kelley Clayton deceased in her Caton home. An investigation revealed her husband, Thomas Clayton, recruited Michael Beard to carry out the crime. Both men were convicted of first degree murder and sentenced to life in prison without parole.

item.title: The Bone-Chilling Story Of Pamela Smart
item.description:
In 1990, Gregory Smart was found deceased in his Derry, New Hampshire, home. Investigators determined that his wife, Pamela Smart, had influenced a teenage acquaintance and his friends to carry out the crime. The case gained national attention as one of the first high-profile trials to be fully televised.
Pamela Smart was convicted of being an accomplice to first-degree murder, conspiracy to commit murder, and witness tampering. She was sentenced to life in prison without the possibility of parole. While the perpetrators of the act have since been released, Smart remains incarcerated as she continues to maintain her innocence through various legal appeals.

media:content.media:title: The Bone-Chilling Story Of Pamela Smart
media:content.media:description:
In 1990, Gregory Smart was found deceased in his Derry, New Hampshire, home. Investigators determined that his wife, Pamela Smart, had influenced a teenage acquaintance and his friends to carry out the crime. The case gained national attention as one of the first high-profile trials to be fully televised.
Pamela Smart was convicted of being an accomplice to first-degree murder, conspiracy to commit murder, and witness tampering. She was sentenced to life in prison without the possibility of parole. While the perpetrators of the act have since been released, Smart remains incarcerated as she continues to maintain her innocence through various legal appeals.
